verdict_reasoning: HSPA5/BiP/GRP78 is canonically an ER-luminal chaperone with a KDEL retention signal, but a well-documented literature establishes that under ER stress conditions (viral infection, tumorigenesis, hypoxia) it translocates to the cell surface (csGRP78). At the cell surface it acts as a receptor/co-receptor involved in viral entry, cancer signalling, and immune modulation. It lacks a transmembrane domain; surface retention appears to involve complex formation with other membrane proteins (e.g., CD109). Surface expression is reproducibly detected by antibodies on living cancer cells and is being pursued as a therapeutic target with antibodies, ADCs, and peptide-drug conjugates in cancer and infectious disease — fulfilling the cell-surface-directed therapeutic criterion. Because this surface presentation is conditional on ER-stress/tumorigenic cell states rather than constitutive, the verdict is contextual rather than yes.
